title: 2. “Is the Room Big Enough for Both These Guys?” | Gene and Roger
format: A narrative doc on how two rival Chicago newspaper critics who could barely share a room became TV's first movie-review double act.

segments.heading: The films that made Gene and Roger
segments.note: the movies threaded through the origin story

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: The stiff, bored early review that opens the episode, proof the writing hadn't yet translated to TV.

films.title: The Devil Within Her
films.note: Roger's "Dog of the Month," panned as an Exorcist rip-off, back when the show was still dry banter.

films.title: Dumbo
films.note: A childhood matinee (Mrs. Jumbo chained up) that stuck with Gene his whole life.

films.title: Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
films.note: Gene's contrarian early call: "laughable, but not very memorable."

films.title: The French Connection
films.note: One of the future classics Gene raved about early at the Tribune.

films.title: Chinatown
films.note: Gene found it "tedious from beginning to just before the end."

films.title: Beyond the Valley of the Dolls
films.note: Gene gave it zero stars and called it a cesspool, not knowing it was written by Roger Ebert.

films.title: Ikiru
films.note: Kurosawa's dying-man drama that hit Roger in college (the transcript mishears it as "Akira").

films.title: The 400 Blows
films.note: Truffaut's coming-of-age film, another Roger college awakening.

films.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
films.note: Roger's lifelong obsession: four stars, and he kept going back. Gene once tried to buy the monolith prop.

films.title: Saturday Night Fever
films.note: Gene's fixation: he saw it 17 times and outbid Jane Fonda for Travolta's white disco suit.

The Devil Within Her.title: I Don't Want to Be Born

Beyond the Valley of the Dolls.title: Beyond the Valley of the Dolls
